items.title: Elegir un nuevo miembro
items.intro: El camino completo desde la solicitud hasta el voto, según los Bylaws.
steps: Verificá la elegibilidad: varón, pasado su 12.º cumpleaños y antes del 21.º, de buen carácter, recomendado por al menos 1 miembro del Chapter o un Senior DeMolay con certificado.
La Membership Application se presenta en una stated meeting, al menos 1 semana antes de votarse (salvo autorización del Executive Officer).
El Master Councilor nombra un committee of investigation de 3 miembros (o el Chapter Advisor / un miembro del Advisory Council investiga en su lugar).
El comité investiga 4 áreas: vida en el hogar, vida social y reputación, historial escolar y laboral — e idealmente visita el hogar.
El informe se presenta POR ESCRITO en o antes de la siguiente stated meeting. La application no puede balotarse sin el informe completo.
Se vota por ballot secreto (ver el procedimiento de balotaje).
Electo el candidato, ninguna elección es final sin la aprobación del Advisory Council.
notes: El informe del comité puede anunciarse pero NO se asienta en actas.
Si el comité no reporta, el MC puede prorrogarlo o nombrar uno nuevo.

items.title: El balotaje (white balls / black cubes)
items.intro: "White balls elect, black cubes reject." Las reglas exactas del voto secreto.
steps: El Senior Deacon inspecciona la obligation card de cada votante, parado entre el altar y la estación del Senior Councilor. Sin tarjeta no se vota.
Orden de votación: MC, SC, JC, Senior Deacon, y luego los miembros desde el Scribe — lado sur primero, después lado norte, desde el West.
El Junior Deacon releva al Sentinel para que también pueda votar.
Resultado: 0 o 1 black cube = electo · 2 black cubes = la application queda en suspenso (lie over) hasta la próxima stated meeting · 3 o más = rechazado.
En el segundo ballot (tras un lie over): electo salvo que aparezcan 3 o más black balls.
Con varios aplicantes se permite el ballot colectivo; si aparecen black balls, se vota por separado cada solicitud.
notes: El voto es privado y el balotaje debe ser rápido; el presiding officer no puede frenar un ballot por pedido privado (hace falta una moción votada por el Chapter).
Objeción después de la elección y antes del Degree: por escrito, firmada por 3 miembros; si la sostiene 1/3 de los presentes, el candidato queda rechazado.
Después de recibir un Degree, las objeciones solo proceden por juicio regular ante el Advisory Council.
Un ballot rechazado se refiere al Advisory Council para más consideración.

items.title: Elegir e instalar oficiales
items.intro: MC, SC y JC se eligen por voto; Scribe y Treasurer los nombra el Advisory Council.
steps: Verificá los requisitos: candidato activo con Obligation Card calificada; para MC, haber sido SC o JC (salvo excepción del Advisory Council o Chapter nuevo).
Se vota con paper ballot, preparado antes de la reunión; gana la mayoría sin contar los votos en blanco.
Los Deacons reparten y recogen los ballots solo a los hermanos que presenten sus obligations; el Marshal asiste en la tabulación.
Empate o falta de mayoría = se vuelve a votar.
La instalación se hace en la misma reunión o dentro de los 30 días.
Vacancia del MC: asciende el SC, luego el JC; el cargo restante se llena por elección con 2 semanas de aviso escrito.
notes: 3 ausencias consecutivas sin excusa aprobada = vacancia automática.
Remoción forzada de un oficial: resolución en una reunión regular + voto de 3/4 de los presentes en la siguiente + aprobación del Executive Officer.

items.title: Manejar el dinero del Chapter
items.intro: Las reglas financieras que no se negocian.
steps: Mantené DOS contabilidades separadas: fondos del Chapter y fondos de DeMolay International (estos últimos son intocables y no requieren voto para pagarse).
El Scribe cobra; el Treasurer recibe y emite recibo al Scribe.
Todo gasto del Chapter (salvo a DI) requiere voto en reunión regular de negocios.
Cheques: firma del Treasurer o del MC + contrafirma de un miembro del Advisory Council.
Pagos electrónicos: todos los aprobadores con acceso de visualización online o copiados automáticamente.
Prohibido pagar en efectivo y prohibido prestar fondos del Chapter.
Inversión de excedentes: voto del Chapter + 2/3 de TODO el Advisory Council por cada inversión.
Auditoría de los libros del Treasurer y el Scribe con cada instalación de un nuevo MC.
notes: Único regalo permitido con fondos del Chapter: el PMC's Jewel.
Solicitar fondos a no-miembros requiere permiso previo del Executive Officer.
Reportes con 3 meses de mora = suspensión temporal automática del Chapter.

items.title: Reportar un incidente
items.intro: Qué hacer cuando algo sale mal en un evento DeMolay.
steps: Atendé primero a las personas; ante daño mayor, lesión seria o muerte, notificá de INMEDIATO al Executive Officer.
Umbral de reporte: toda ocurrencia con potencial de lesión o daño a propiedad que supere los $100.
Registrá los hechos: WHEN, WHERE, WHAT (daño/lesión), WHO, si se llamó a la policía, y sacá fotos.
Cadena de reporte: Chapter → Advisory Council Chairman / Chapter Advisor → Executive Officer → Service and Leadership Center → aseguradora.
En propiedad masónica: el Chairman da notificación ORAL al cuerpo masónico, registrando fecha, hora y destinatario.
Completá el incident report form y conservá copia de todos los documentos.
notes: NO hagas declaraciones de responsabilidad en la escena ni alientes reclamos: solo hechos, no opiniones.
Ante investigadores de seguros: contactá al Executive Officer ANTES de hablar.
El seguro de DeMolay es SECUNDARIO al seguro familiar y solo cubre eventos documentados en minutas.

items.title: Reportar sospecha de abuso
items.intro: Para reportar basta la sospecha: no hace falta prueba. La protección va primero.
steps: Garantizá la seguridad inmediata del joven.
Llamá al 911 o a la agencia local de protección infantil.
Notificá al Jurisdictional Executive Officer dentro de las 24 horas, con reporte escrito.
Si el presunto perpetrador es un adulto del programa: queda en licencia (leave) hasta que termine la investigación.
Cooperá con las autoridades y preservá toda la información.
notes: Regla permanente: Two-Deep Leadership (mínimo 2 registered Advisors) y nada de actividades one-on-one.
Los formularios de Medical Release y Photo Release están en beademolay.org/resources.

items.title: Fundar un Chapter: la check-off list oficial (17 pasos)
items.intro: La lista de control completa del manual para organizar un Chapter nuevo. Tildá a medida que avances: tu progreso queda guardado en este dispositivo.
steps: Hablar con el Executive Officer (o su representante personal) sobre establecer un Chapter.
Encontrar otros Masons interesados (montar un programa informativo).
Asegurar un Sponsoring Body y que apruebe una Resolution to Sponsor.
Seleccionar un Advisory Council y que complete el training online.
Elegir el nombre del Chapter (prohibido el nombre de una persona viva).
Completar la Petition for Letters Temporary.
Enviar al Executive Officer: Resolution to Sponsor + Advisory Council Certification + Petition for Letters Temporary + cheque por la fee.
Recibir las Letters Temporary del Executive Officer.
Revisar el material recibido y pedir los supplies adicionales necesarios.
Establecer la membership fee del Chapter.
Organizar el evento de membresía para la clase inicial.
Registrar y aprobar las applications for membership.
Inducir a la clase inicial (con uno o más Chapters visitantes para los Degrees).
Reportar los nuevos Members a DeMolay International online (máximo 10 días).
Seleccionar e instalar al primer grupo de Chapter officers.
Adoptar el set de Chapter Bylaws (aprobados por el Executive Officer).
Operar el Chapter según sus bylaws, las regulaciones y las políticas jurisdiccionales.
notes: El Chapter opera bajo Letters Temporary hasta tener al menos 15 Members.
Para reinstalar un Chapter cerrado: mismo procedimiento, pero con una reinstatement application en lugar de la Petition for Letters Temporary.

items.title: Electing a new member
items.intro: The full path from application to vote, per the Bylaws.
steps: Check eligibility: a young man past his 12th birthday and not yet 21, of good character, recommended by at least 1 Chapter member or a certified Senior DeMolay.
The Membership Application is presented at a stated meeting, at least 1 week before being voted on (unless the Executive Officer authorizes otherwise).
The Master Councilor appoints a 3-member committee of investigation (or the Chapter Advisor / an Advisory Council member investigates instead).
The committee investigates 4 areas: home life, social life and reputation, school record and employment record — ideally with a home visit.
The report is submitted IN WRITING at or before the next stated meeting. The application cannot be balloted without the complete report.
The vote is taken by secret ballot (see the balloting procedure).
Once elected, no election is final without the Advisory Council's approval.
notes: The committee's report may be announced but is NOT entered in the minutes.
If the committee fails to report, the MC may continue it or appoint a new one.

items.title: The ballot (white balls / black cubes)
items.intro: "White balls elect, black cubes reject." The exact rules of the secret vote.
steps: The Senior Deacon inspects each voter's obligation card, standing between the altar and the Senior Councilor's station. No card, no vote.
Voting order: MC, SC, JC, Senior Deacon, then the members starting from the Scribe — south side first, then north side, from the West.
The Junior Deacon relieves the Sentinel so he can vote too.
Result: 0 or 1 black cube = elected · 2 black cubes = the application lies over to the next stated meeting · 3 or more = rejected.
On the second ballot (after a lie-over): elected unless 3 or more black balls appear.
With several applicants a collective ballot is allowed; if black balls appear, each application is balloted separately.
notes: The vote is private and balloting should be brisk; the presiding officer cannot stop an ordered ballot on a private request (it takes a motion voted by the Chapter).
Objection after election and before the Degree: written, signed by 3 members; if sustained by 1/3 of those present, the candidate is rejected.
After a Degree is received, objections proceed only via regular trial before the Advisory Council.
A rejected ballot is referred to the Advisory Council for further consideration.

items.title: Electing & installing officers
items.intro: MC, SC and JC are elected by ballot; Scribe and Treasurer are appointed by the Advisory Council.
steps: Check requirements: an active candidate with a qualified Obligation Card; for MC, prior service as SC or JC (exceptions via the Advisory Council or in a new Chapter).
Vote by paper ballot, prepared before the meeting; a majority wins, blank votes don't count.
The Deacons hand out and collect ballots only from brothers presenting their obligations; the Marshal assists with tabulation.
Tie or no majority = revote.
Installation happens at the same meeting or within 30 days.
MC vacancy: the SC moves up, then the JC; the remaining office is filled by election with 2 weeks' written notice.
notes: 3 consecutive unexcused absences = automatic vacancy.
Forced removal of an officer: a resolution at a regular meeting + a 3/4 vote of those present at the next one + the Executive Officer's approval.

items.title: Handling Chapter money
items.intro: The financial rules that are not negotiable.
steps: Keep TWO separate sets of books: Chapter funds and DeMolay International funds (the latter are untouchable and need no vote to be paid).
The Scribe collects; the Treasurer receives and gives the Scribe a receipt.
Every Chapter expense (except to DI) requires a vote at a regular business meeting.
Checks: signed by the Treasurer or the MC + countersigned by an Advisory Council member.
Electronic payments: every approver gets online viewing access or automatic copies.
Cash payments are prohibited, and lending Chapter funds is prohibited.
Investing surplus: a Chapter vote + 2/3 of the ENTIRE Advisory Council for each investment.
The Treasurer's and Scribe's books are audited at each new MC's installation.
notes: The only gift Chapter funds may buy: the PMC's Jewel.
Soliciting funds from non-members requires the Executive Officer's prior permission.
Reports 3 months overdue = automatic temporary suspension of the Chapter.

items.title: Reporting an incident
items.intro: What to do when something goes wrong at a DeMolay event.
steps: People first; for major damage, serious injury or death, notify the Executive Officer IMMEDIATELY.
Reporting threshold: any occurrence with potential for bodily injury or property damage over $100.
Record the facts: WHEN, WHERE, WHAT (damage/injury), WHO, whether police were called — and take photos.
Reporting chain: Chapter → Advisory Council Chairman / Chapter Advisor → Executive Officer → Service and Leadership Center → insurer.
On Masonic property: the Chairman gives ORAL notification to the Masonic body, recording date, time and recipient.
Complete the incident report form and keep copies of every document.
notes: Do NOT make statements of responsibility at the scene or encourage claims: facts only, no opinions.
If insurance investigators call: contact the Executive Officer BEFORE talking.
DeMolay's insurance is SECONDARY to family insurance and only covers events documented in the minutes.

items.title: Reporting suspected abuse
items.intro: Suspicion is enough to report — no proof needed. Protection comes first.
steps: Ensure the young person's immediate safety.
Call 911 or the local child protection agency.
Notify the Jurisdictional Executive Officer within 24 hours, with a written report.
If the alleged perpetrator is a program adult: he is placed on leave until the investigation ends.
Cooperate with authorities and preserve all information.
notes: Standing rule: Two-Deep Leadership (minimum 2 registered Advisors) and no one-on-one activities.
Medical Release and Photo Release forms live at beademolay.org/resources.

items.title: Starting a Chapter: the official check-off list (17 steps)
items.intro: The handbook's complete checklist for organizing a new Chapter. Check items off as you go — your progress is saved on this device.
steps: Talk to the Executive Officer (or his personal representative) about establishing a Chapter.
Find other interested Masons (set up an informational program).
Secure a Sponsoring Body and have it pass a Resolution to Sponsor.
Select an Advisory Council and have it complete the online training.
Choose the Chapter name (no living person's name allowed).
Complete the Petition for Letters Temporary.
Send the Executive Officer: Resolution to Sponsor + Advisory Council Certification + Petition for Letters Temporary + the fee check.
Receive the Letters Temporary from the Executive Officer.
Review the materials received and order any additional supplies needed.
Set the Chapter's membership fee.
Organize the membership event for the initial class.
Record and approve the applications for membership.
Induct the initial class (with one or more visiting Chapters for the Degrees).
Report the new Members to DeMolay International online (10 days max).
Select and install the first group of Chapter officers.
Adopt the set of Chapter Bylaws (approved by the Executive Officer).
Operate the Chapter per its bylaws, regulations and jurisdictional policies.
notes: The Chapter operates under Letters Temporary until it has at least 15 Members.
To reinstate a closed Chapter: same procedure, but with a reinstatement application instead of the Petition for Letters Temporary.
